description: Frozen-LP vs FT BA matrix + HP sweep leaderboard + Lin-compare runs. Snapshot from 2026-04-09 session.

frozen_lp_vs_ft_matrix.note: Frozen LP = sklearn LogisticRegression on mean-pooled 200-d LaBraM features, subject-level StratifiedGroupKFold(5), class_weight=balanced. Multi-seed mean ± std over seeds [42,123,2024].

stress_per_rec_dass.label_source: data/comprehensive_labels.csv Group column (per-recording, matches Lin 2025 protocol)

stress_subject_dass_oragg.label_source: OR-aggregated across each subject's recordings (our canonical --label subject-dass mode)
stress_subject_dass_oragg.classification: Trait memorization artifact — FT helps only because label becomes subject-identity broadcast

adftd.label_source: HC vs AD from ADFTD dataset

tdbrain.classification: Silent erosion — BA 打平但 representation label fraction 掉 50%

eegmat.classification: MILD INJECTION (reclassified from no-op 2026-04-09 — BA gain confirms FT adds signal)

hp_sweep_20260409_main_leaderboard.note: All 126 runs used COMMON_ARGS with --label subject-dass. Per-model best config by mean BA over 3 seeds. REVE re-ran 2026-04-09 afternoon after original sweep was killed by UI crash.
hp_sweep_20260409_main_leaderboard.caveat: Canonical LaBraM historical value is 0.6559 (same HP cell as sweep's 2nd-best), not reproduced by today's sweep due to code drift since 2026-04-05. Root cause still unresolved as of 2026-04-09 evening.

lin_2025_comparison.paper: Wang, Zhang, Chen, Truong, Jung — arXiv:2505.23042 (2025)
lin_2025_comparison.lin_protocol: Per-recording DASS labels, 80/10/10 by-recording split (not by-subject), 5s windows with 75% overlap, patches shuffled within each set before exporting pickles

our_strict_equivalent.protocol: Per-recording DASS (Group column), subject-level StratifiedGroupKFold(5), same FT recipe as canonical LaBraM 0.6559
our_strict_equivalent.gap_vs_lin_avg: ~26 pp, entirely attributable to split protocol + label aggregation
our_strict_equivalent.observation: Frozen LP BEATS FT under strict evaluation — Stress is an erosion dataset.

key_reclassifications_2026_04_09: Stress (per-rec dass) moved from 'no-op' → 'erosion': frozen LP (0.62) > FT (0.54) by 6-8 pp
Stress subject-dass 0.66 reinterpreted as 'trait memorization artifact' rather than 'ceiling': FT only helps because OR-aggregated label is de facto subject-ID broadcast
EEGMAT moved from 'no-op' → 'mild injection': FT (0.74) > frozen LP (0.67), +6.5 pp
TDBRAIN erosion confirmed behaviorally: frozen LP (0.68) ≈ FT (0.68), paired with -1.5 pp label fraction
REVE moved from 'ruled out / chance-ish' → 'top sweep performer on subject-dass': 0.70 (but high seed variance ±7 pp, and subject-dass interpretation caveat applies)
CBraMod similarly from 'ruled out 0.488' → '0.62 sweep best' (label mode note: sweep is subject-dass)
followups_pending: Multi-seed confirmation of Stress FT --label dass (need seeds 123, 2024 — today only ran seed 42)
Permutation null for Stress per-rec dass Δ (does -0.08 exceed what label-shuffle FT would produce?)
Recompute paper/figures/variance_analysis.json Stress entry using per-rec dass labels (currently uses subject-dass)
CBraMod / REVE repeat with --label dass to check if their sweep numbers are also trait-memorization artifacts
Code drift root cause — what changed between 2026-04-05 canonical and 2026-04-09 sweep that dropped LaBraM 0.656 → 0.591 at identical HP
HNC MDD replication of erosion mode (pending HDF5 decryption + integration)
Stress FT feature extraction with --label dass to enable pooled label fraction recompute
